clusters.title: Trump weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump considers seizing Iran oil terminal

items.title: Trump weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
items.standfirst: The president said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.
items.content: The president said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)

items.title: Brent crude hits $116 a barrel after Trump says he wants to ‘take the oil in Iran’
items.standfirst: Stock markets in Asia drop sharply, with investors nervous over escalation of Middle East conflictBusiness live – latest newsThe price of oil has risen sharply to $116 a barrel after Donald Trump said he wanted to “take the oil in Iran”, sending Asian stock markets tumbling.Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, rose by 2% in early trading on Monday, after Trump said that he could seize the Iranian export hub of Kharg Island. Continue reading...
items.content: Stock markets in Asia drop sharply, with investors nervous over escalation of Middle East conflictBusiness live – latest newsThe price of oil has risen sharply to $116 a barrel after Donald Trump said he wanted to “take the oil in Iran”, sending Asian stock markets tumbling.Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, rose by 2% in early trading on Monday, after Trump said that he could seize the Iranian export hub of Kharg Island. Continue reading...

items.title: What did Trump say about taking Iran oil? #politics
items.standfirst: Trump floated seizing Iran’s export hub President Donald Trump said he would like to “take the oil in Iran” and is considering seizing the export hub of Kharg Island, which is responsible for more than 90% of Iran’s oil exports. Why it matters That prospect would represent a major shift from…

Why it matters That prospect would represent a major shift from…

items.title: Trump says he wants Iran's oil and could seize Kharg Island
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Sunday that he would like to "take the oil in Iran" and is considering seizing the export hub of Kharg Island, which is responsible for more than 90% of Iran's oil exports
items.content: President Donald Trump said Sunday that he would like to "take the oil in Iran" and is considering seizing the export hub of Kharg Island, which is responsible for more than 90% of Iran's oil exports

items.title: Trump says US could ‘take the oil in Iran’
items.standfirst: US president tells the FT he is considering seizing strategic Kharg Island even as negotiations continue
items.content: US president tells the FT he is considering seizing strategic Kharg Island even as negotiations continue

ai_summary: Trump wants to take Iran's oil
Kharg Island is Iran's main oil export hub
Brent crude price rises to $116 a barrel
Trump considers sending US forces to Iran

clusters.title: Thieves grab paintings by 3 masters from Italian museum in under 3 minutes
clusters.neutral_headline: Thieves steal paintings from Italian museum

items.title: Thieves grab paintings by 3 masters from Italian museum in under 3 minutes
items.standfirst: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions from an Italian museum in under three minutes, reports say.
items.content: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions from an Italian museum in under three minutes, reports say.

items.title: Paintings 'worth millions' stolen from museum in Italy
items.standfirst: Three famous paintings worth millions have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy.
items.content: Three famous paintings worth millions have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy.

items.title: Thieves steal Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse paintings worth millions from Italian museum
items.standfirst: Four masked men entered Magnani Rocca Foundation villa, near Parma in northern Italy, and made off with artworksThieves stole paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse from a museum in Italy a week ago, police have said.Four masked men entered the villa of the Magnani Rocca Foundation, near Parma in northern Italy, and made off with the artworks on the night of 22 March, a police spokesperson said, confirming a report on the Rai television network. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Thieves steal Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse paintings in three-minute Italian heist
items.standfirst: The paintings were taken from a museum in Parma by four masked men in the middle of the night, police say.
items.content: The paintings were taken from a museum in Parma by four masked men in the middle of the night, police say.

ai_summary: Thieves stole three paintings in under three minutes

clusters.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel to expand operations in Lebanon

items.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
items.standfirst: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
items.content: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)

items.title: Israel to expand operations in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu says he has instructed an expansion of Israel's operations in southern Lebanon. This, as Israeli forces continue to target the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group. And with the Lebanese government now saying 1,200 people have been killed in the country due to fighting that began at the start of March.
items.content: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu says he has instructed an expansion of Israel's operations in southern Lebanon. This, as Israeli forces continue to target the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group. And with the Lebanese government now saying 1,200 people have been killed in the country due to fighting that began at the start of March.

items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday that Israel’s military will widen its ground campaign in southern Lebanon

clusters.neutral_headline: Latin Patriarch regains access to Holy Sepulchre

items.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
items.standfirst: The Israeli PM said Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church out of concern for his safety.
items.content: The Israeli PM said Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church out of concern for his safety.

items.title: Israel restores Latin Patriarch’s access to Holy Sepulchre church after international outcry
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem now has "full and immediate access" to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa was blocked from holding Palm Sunday Mass at Christianity's holiest site, triggering an international outcry.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem now has "full and immediate access" to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa was blocked from holding Palm Sunday Mass at Christianity's holiest site, triggering an international outcry.

items.title: Israeli police block Catholic figures from Palm Sunday Mass at Jerusalem's Holy Sepulchre
items.standfirst: World leaders have voiced concern after Israeli police prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa, the Latin patriarch of Jerusalem, from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre on Sunday
items.content: World leaders have voiced concern after Israeli police prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa, the Latin patriarch of Jerusalem, from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre on Sunday

ai_summary: Israel restores Latin Patriarch's access to church
Cardinal Pizzaballa had been blocked from entry

clusters.title: Murder trial opens over alleged masonic lodge crime network in Paris
clusters.neutral_headline: Murder trial opens for French masonic lodge

items.title: Murder trial opens over alleged masonic lodge crime network in Paris
items.standfirst: Twenty-two defendants, including intelligence agents and police, accused of committing crimes on behalf of Freemason mafiaTwenty-two people are to stand trial in France from Monday on charges of murder and other serious crimes centred on a masonic lodge accused of running hit squads.Seven defendants – including former intelligence agents, soldiers and businessmen – face possible life sentences. Prosecutors allege the group carried out murder, attempted murder, aggravated assault and criminal conspiracy on behalf of a mafia network inside the Athanor lodge in the Paris suburb of Puteaux. Continue reading...
items.content: Twenty-two defendants, including intelligence agents and police, accused of committing crimes on behalf of Freemason mafiaTwenty-two people are to stand trial in France from Monday on charges of murder and other serious crimes centred on a masonic lodge accused of running hit squads.Seven defendants – including former intelligence agents, soldiers and businessmen – face possible life sentences. Prosecutors allege the group carried out murder, attempted murder, aggravated assault and criminal conspiracy on behalf of a mafia network inside the Athanor lodge in the Paris suburb of Puteaux. Continue reading...

items.title: Murder trial involving Freemasons, French secret agents opens in Paris court
items.standfirst: A Paris court on Monday began hearings in a major trial involving 22 suspects accused of murder and other serious crimes on behalf of a mafia network inside the Athanor Masonic Lodge in the Paris suburb of Puteaux. The accused include police officers, former French intelligence agents and businessmen.
items.content: A Paris court on Monday began hearings in a major trial involving 22 suspects accused of murder and other serious crimes on behalf of a mafia network inside the Athanor Masonic Lodge in the Paris suburb of Puteaux. The accused include police officers, former French intelligence agents and businessmen.

ai_summary: Twenty-two defendants face murder charges
Masonic lodge accused of running hit squads
Former intelligence agents among defendants
Trial begins in Paris court
Defendants face possible life sentences
